steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that tool similarities could stem from imitation or trade, then presents a key finding from Altınışık et al.: "genetic affinity among the populations." The correct answer must use that genetic affinity evidence to support one specific conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "It suggests that in the Neolithic period, people in the South Levant and Central Anatolia imitated tool designs originating in Cayönü Tepesi more frequently than people in Central Zagros did.": Genetic affinity shows biological connection, not imitation; this misuses the evidence. ✗
  • "It helps explain why contemporaneous Neolithic peoples in Southwest Asia had similar tools but lacked other obvious cultural similarities.": The passage draws no conclusions about which group migrated first. ✗
  • "It raises the possibility that similarities in the design of tools found at Neolithic settlements in Southwest Asia emerged due to population blending between those settlements.": Genetic affinity directly supports population blending as the explanation, which the passage explicitly contrasts with imitation and trade. ✓
  • "It implies that people and tools likely arrived in Cayönü Tepesi from settlements Central Anatolia and the South Levant at an earlier time than they did from settlements in Central Zagros.": The passage does not discuss cultural differences between the populations. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
